disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Racing heart, lightheadedness and near-fainting come on within a few minutes of standing and settle almost immediately on lying flat
trigger_question_examples: When exactly do the symptoms come on?
What happens when you lie back down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It only happens when I stand up or stay upright — within a couple of minutes my heart is pounding and I feel faint. The moment I lie back down it settles.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On standing the wearable heart rate rises from roughly 75 to 125-140 with no reported blackout
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to measure your heart rate?
What does your smartwatch show when you stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My watch says about 75 lying down and it shoots up to around 125 to 140 within a few minutes of standing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reddish-purple discoloration of the lower legs and feet develops on standing and resolves on lying down
trigger_question_examples: Do you notice any colour change in your legs?
Does anything visibly change when you stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah — after a few minutes standing my lower legs and feet go a reddish-purple, and it disappears when I lie down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic fatigue, marked exercise intolerance and 'brain fog' alongside the postural symptoms
trigger_question_examples: How have your energy levels and concentration been?
Can you exercise or exert yourself normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm exhausted all the time, I can't handle any exercise, and my head feels foggy and slow.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No true loss of consciousness or fall has occurred — presyncope only
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever actually passed out or fallen?
Have you lost consciousness at any point?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I've never actually blacked out or fallen — I just feel like I'm about to faint.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The racing heart does not persist while lying flat — it does not continue at rest or wake her from sleep
trigger_question_examples: Does your heart ever race while you're lying still or asleep?
Does the fast heart rate keep going after you lie down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — when I lie down the racing stops pretty quickly. It doesn't happen while I'm resting or asleep.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and she has been trying to drink lots of water, with multiple bottles by the bed
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain with the episodes?
How much are you managing to drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain. I've been trying to drink heaps of water — there are bottles all around my bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe functional impact and high anxiety — currently bedbound and distressed about university
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your day-to-day life?
How are you coping with it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's ruining everything — I'm stuck in bed, I can't study, and I'm really anxious about falling behind at uni.
